articles.title: Jharkhand braces for monsoon between June 7-10: Heatwave continues in 3 districts, CM directs officials to ... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Jharkhand braces for monsoon between June 7-10:Heatwave continues in 3 districts, CM directs officials to ensure water supply
Jharkhand heatwave intensifies, Medininagar hottest at 44.8°C; rain and thunderstorm alert in several districts
Severe heat has disrupted normal life across Jharkhand, with temperatures in most districts ranging between 35°C and 45°C. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for several districts, while some parts of the state may also witness rain, thunderstorms and strong winds over the next few days.
Ranchi records 39.2°C, Medininagar hottest
In the last 24 hours, the maximum temperature in Ranchi was recorded at 39.2°C. According to the Meteorological Department, the temperature in the capital may touch 40°C on Saturday and could rise to 41°C on May 25 and 26.
Medininagar remained the hottest place in the state at 44.8°C. Other major temperature readings included:
Koderma – 41.0°C
Hazaribag – 40.6°C
Lohardaga – 40.0°C
Khunti – 39.4°C
Gumla – 39.0°C
The intense heat and humidity have increased the risk of dehydration and heatstroke. People have been advised to avoid stepping out during afternoon hours unless necessary.
Heatwave alert in Palamu, Chatra and Garhwa
The Meteorological Department has issued an orange alert for heatwave conditions in Palamu, Chatra and Garhwa for today and tomorrow. Temperatures are expected to rise further in these districts.
At the same time, weather conditions are expected to remain unstable in several other parts of the state.
Rain and thunderstorm forecast in several districts
Between May 24 and 25, light to moderate rainfall, thunderstorms and strong winds are likely in north-eastern and central Jharkhand.
Warnings for thunderstorms with wind speeds of 50 to 60 kmph have been issued for:
On May 26 and 27, southern and central districts including Jamshedpur, Chaibasa and Saraikela-Kharsawan may receive rain accompanied by thunder and gusty winds. However, no major drop in temperature is expected.
The possibility of light rain and thunderstorms is also likely to continue at isolated places on May 28 and 29.
CM directs officials to ensure drinking water supply
Amid the severe heatwave, Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren has directed officials to ensure uninterrupted drinking water supply across the state.
He instructed all DCs, SPs, COs, BDOs, police station in-charges and panchayat representatives to remain alert and make arrangements for clean drinking water at government offices and public places.
The Chief Minister said no village or locality should face water shortage. He ordered immediate tanker supply in affected areas, repair of defunct hand pumps and alternative arrangements wherever needed.
Monsoon likely to enter Jharkhand between June 7 and 10
According to IMD, the southwest monsoon has already reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and conditions remain favourable for its further advance. The monsoon is likely to reach Kerala by May 26.
After that, it is expected to enter Jharkhand between June 7 and 10 through the Santhal Pargana region.
This year, temperatures in Jharkhand during May have been recorded 5 to 7 degrees higher than last year. Areas that earlier recorded 36°C to 38°C are now witnessing temperatures between 40°C and 45°C, forcing residents to endure prolonged heat and hot winds.
